executive_summary.description: A high-level overview of the methods available to integrate flowchart and system diagramming capabilities into a terminal-based Claude Code workflow, summarizing the key approaches and recommendations.

path_name.description: The name of the recommended implementation path.

reasoning.description: The justification for why this path is the best overall recommendation.

setup_command.description: A one-line command to set up the required tools for this path.

workflow_description.description: A brief description of the end-to-end workflow for this path.

path_name.description: The name of the recommended lightweight fallback path.

reasoning.description: The justification for why this path is the best lightweight fallback.

setup_command.description: A one-line command to set up the required tools for this path.

workflow_description.description: A brief description of the end-to-end workflow for this path.

path_name.description: The name or number of the implementation path.

setup_command.description: A one-line command to install the necessary tools for this path.

workflow.description: Description of the prompt-to-diagram flow for this path.

display_method.description: How the final diagram is displayed (e.g., TUI ASCII, web-view SVG).

pros.description: The advantages of this implementation path.

cons.description: The disadvantages or limitations of this implementation path.

best_fit_use_case.description: The ideal scenario or use case for this path (e.g., quick sketches, large graphs, CI).

recommendation_matrix.description: A detailed matrix of 3-5 concrete, end-to-end implementation paths a user can take. Each path will include a name, a one-line setup command, a description of the prompt-to-diagram workflow, pros, cons, and the best-fit use case (e.g., quick sketches, large graphs, offline use, CI).

tool_name.description: The name of the diagramming tool or engine.

input_dsl.description: The input language or Domain-Specific Language used by the tool (e.g., Mermaid, D2, DOT).

output_formats.description: The supported output formats (e.g., SVG, PNG, PDF, ASCII).

installation_command.description: A one-line command for installing the tool.

llm_suitability.description: Analysis of the tool's syntax tolerance and suitability for processing LLM-generated code.

layout_quality.description: Description of the layout engine's quality and customization options.

licensing.description: The software license for the tool (e.g., MIT, GPL).

cli_tui_diagramming_engines.description: A comprehensive comparison of existing CLI/TUI diagramming tools and rendering engines. Each entry will detail the tool's input DSL, output formats (SVG, PNG, ASCII), installation command, syntax tolerance for LLM output, layout quality, and rendering paths (TUI vs. web-view).

tool_name.description: The name of the code analysis or auto-generation tool.

supported_languages.description: The programming languages supported by the tool.

graph_types_generated.description: The types of graphs the tool can generate (e.g., call graph, dependency graph, UML).

export_formats.description: The diagram DSLs or formats the tool can export to (e.g., Mermaid, D2, DOT, JSON).

cli_usability.description: An example command and notes on the tool's command-line interface usability.

licensing_and_cost.description: The licensing model and any associated costs (e.g., MIT, Commercial).

code_analysis_and_autogen_tools.description: An analysis of tools that automatically generate flow diagrams or dependency graphs from codebases. Each tool's entry will cover supported languages, types of graphs generated (call graph, dependency), accuracy, export pathways to diagram DSLs (Mermaid, D2, DOT), and CLI usability.

pattern_name.description: The name of the integration pattern (e.g., Custom Slash Command, MCP Server).

architecture_description.description: A description of the architectural components and data flow for this pattern.

implementation_example.description: A minimal reference implementation or pseudo-code for the pattern.

refresh_strategy.description: How diagrams are updated (e.g., static one-off, live-reloading on save).

security_considerations.description: Notes on security, sandboxing, and permissions for this pattern.

claude_code_integration_patterns.description: A breakdown of specific patterns for integrating diagramming into Claude Code. This includes architectural details and examples for custom slash commands, subagents for code analysis, MCP servers for rendering, artifact-style web views, and hooks for auto-generation.

technology_name.description: The name of the terminal rendering technology (e.g., Kitty Graphics Protocol, Sixel, Chafa).

description.description: A brief description of what the technology does.

primary_supported_terminals.description: A list of terminal emulators that have native or primary support for this technology.

fallback_mechanism.description: The fallback rendering method used when the primary protocol is unavailable (e.g., ANSI Block Characters, ASCII).

terminal_rendering_technologies.description: An overview of methods for rendering graphical output directly in the terminal. This covers terminal-native image protocols (Kitty, iTerm2, sixel) and image-to-text converters (chafa, timg), including a compatibility matrix for different terminal emulators and fallbacks.

llm_native_tools_summary.description: Summary of LLM-native diagramming and AI-powered code visualization tools like CodeSee, Swimm, and AppMap.

modern_tui_frameworks_summary.description: Summary of new TUI frameworks like Ratatui, Bubble Tea, and Textual and their graph rendering capabilities.

cli_adaptation_feasibility.description: Analysis of the feasibility of adapting VS Code extensions or other GUI tools for a pure CLI workflow.

tui_interactivity_vs_webview_fidelity.description: Comparison of TUI interactivity (low) versus web view fidelity (high).

static_vs_live_updating.description: Comparison of static, one-off diagram generation versus live-updating pipelines.

filesize_vs_rendering_latency.description: Comparison of output file size versus the time it takes to render the diagram for different formats.

dsl_name.description: The name of the Diagram-Specific Language (e.g., Mermaid, D2, Graphviz).

syntax_forgiveness.description: How well the DSL and its renderer handle minor syntax errors, which are common in LLM-generated output.

layout_robustness.description: The quality and robustness of the automatic layout engine, especially for complex graphs without manual tuning.

overall_llm_suitability.description: A summary of the DSL's overall suitability for a 'prompt-to-diagram' workflow driven by an LLM.

terminal_emulator_compatibility.description: A summary of compatibility across different terminal emulators (Kitty, iTerm2, etc.) and their support for image protocols.

tmux_and_ssh_caveats.description: Considerations and workarounds for using diagramming tools within tmux, screen, or over an SSH connection.

os_packaging_and_offline_use.description: Guidance on OS-specific installation and caching packages for airgapped or offline environments.

licensing_and_compliance.description: An overview of licensing and redistribution concerns for tools like PlantUML and proprietary analyzers.

security_hardening_practices.description: Recommended security practices, such as using Docker, user namespaces, and resource limits to isolate rendering processes.
